title: notifications core — kill switches + rollout controls
description: Runtime toggles resolved via Laravel Pennant. Kill switches default ON — flipping OFF is an incident-response action. Rollout flags default OFF and progress via a Pennant scope function (percentage / cohort / tenant).

flags.description: Master kill switch — disables SendNotificationJob dispatch. Every dispatch call is short-circuited into a suppressed NotificationDelivery. Used only during a total-notifications outage. Every drop is audit-logged with reason `dispatch_kill_switch`.
Disables digest batching. In-flight digests deliver; new digest-mode notifications route to immediate dispatch instead. Used when the digest scheduler misbehaves.
Disables tenant-override template resolution. All sends fall back to the platform-default template. Used when a tenant template is malformed + causing a delivery-failure spike.

Disables the Reverb broadcast on user.{id}.notifications + tenant.{id}.notifications. The database write for in-app notifications still happens — the bell just doesn't live-update. Used during Reverb incidents; UX degrades to poll-on-refresh.
Disables the preference cache. Every PreferenceResolver::resolve() call hits the DB directly. Emergency toggle for cache corruption.
Enables a per-tenant dispatch rate limit (default 1000/min) to prevent a runaway loop in one tenant from starving others. Rolled out per-tenant via a Pennant scope function.
